summary: Adversarially audit ChatGPT high-accuracy 28-stroke squaring variant: geometry, stroke accounting, legality, and frontier placement

findings.claim: Step 1: c(B,BO) and c(Q,QO) meet at a second point C=(1,1) distinct from O; BC is x=1, tangent to GAMMA at B
findings.detail: Center distance d(B,Q)=sqrt(2) with radii 1,1 satisfies 0=|r1-r2|<sqrt(2)<2=r1+r2, so two distinct intersections exist. Recomputed: {(0,0), (1,1)} — one candidate is O itself (numerical distance 1.2e-61), the other is C=(1,1) at distance sqrt(2) from O. 'Second intersection' is unambiguous because O is an already-named point. Line BC recomputed vertical (direction (0,1)), dist(O, line BC)=1.000... = r, so tangent at B. BC=1 exactly.

findings.claim: Step 2: E=(-3,0) free intersection, BE=4r
findings.detail: c(P,2) x diameter line: center P on the line, disc=16>0, intersections {(1,0)=B, (-3,0)}. Selector doubly resolved: 'again' excludes existing point B, and 'beyond P' checks out (dot((E-P),(B-P))<0). BE=4.000... exactly. Both objects (circle and diameter LINE) are drawn in the base state, so 0 strokes is legitimate.

findings.claim: Step 3: D=(-9/2,0) with BD=11r/2; selector 'beyond E' is load-bearing and stated
findings.detail: PX recomputed = 3/2. c(E,3/2) x diameter: center E on the line, disc=9>0, intersections {(-9/2,0), (-3/2,0)}. 'Beyond E' selects (-9/2,0) unambiguously (the other candidate (-3/2,0) lies BETWEEN B and E, not beyond). LOAD-BEARING: wrong branch gives BD'=5/2 -> BU=1/20, BV=1/400, final relative side error -9.52e-7 instead of -6.04e-12 (five orders worse). Circle is a rigid-compass distance transfer (E, P, X all constructed) = 1 legal stroke per the stated metric.

findings.claim: Step 4: parallel to DC through G meets BC at U=(1,1/44); BU=r/44
findings.detail: DC direction (11/2,1) vs vertical BC direction (0,1): cross product = 11/2 != 0, lines not parallel — intersection exists and is unique (no selector). Parallelogram expansion c(C,|GD|) x c(G,|CD|) recomputed: two points exist (d=1.0078, non-degenerate since dist(G, line DC)=0.9615>0); correct branch M=(51/8,1) gives GM || DC; U=(1, 0.0227272727...) = (1, 1/44) exact to 60 dps. Similarity ratio BG/BD=(1/8)/(11/2)=1/44 confirmed. CAVEAT: the expansion's own two-point selector (parallelogram vs crossed antiparallel, wrong branch gives U=(1,-0.0571)) is not stated in the prose — figure-resolvable and standard, but load-bearing within the expansion.

findings.claim: Step 5: parallel to DU through G meets BC at V=(1,1/1936); BV=r/44^2
findings.detail: DU direction (11/2, 1/44) vs vertical BC: cross = 11/2 != 0, not parallel; unique intersection. dist(G, line DU)=0.0222>0 so the parallelogram expansion is non-degenerate (both circles intersect). V recomputed = (1, 0.00051652892561983471074) = (1, 1/1936) exact. Second similarity BV/BU = BG/BD = 1/44 verified numerically.

findings.claim: Step 6: J on line OW with WJ=BV exists; selector above/below W unstated but harmless
findings.detail: c(W,BV) x line OW: center W lies on OW so two intersections always exist (disc=8.004e-7>0): J=(0, sqrt(3)-1/1936) or (0, sqrt(3)+1/1936). The prose does NOT choose. Both branches tested end-to-end: final JL identical to all 60 digits (JL^2=WI^2-WJ^2 depends only on |WJ|=1/1936). Harmless-by-symmetry. Line OW is drawn in the base; W, B, V all constructed; the mark is correctly costed as a 1-stroke distance transfer, not treated as free. J=(0,1.73153427864325745881670253985) [below branch]. The parallel-to-OB through J is correctly costed as 3 expanded strokes; J not on line OB (offset ~1.7315>0) so expansion non-degenerate.

findings.claim: Step 7: c(W,WI) reaches the horizontal line through J and meets it at L; JL^2 = WI^2 - BV^2
findings.detail: Radius WI = sqrt(355/113) = 1.77245392615830... vs vertical offset |y_J - y_W| = 1/1936 = 5.165e-4; ratio ~3431, disc = 3.14159...>0 — two intersections with huge margin, both J-branches. L = (+-1.77245385089481280375890373052, y_J). Left/right selector unstated but harmless-by-symmetry: J is at x=0 and JL=|x_L| is identical both sides (tested all 4 J/L branch combinations — same JL to 60 digits). Collapsing-compass legality of c(W,WI) is genuine: circle centered at W through the existing point I needs no transfer; segment WI being undrawn is irrelevant. L lies on the step-6 parallel, so 'no extra stroke for JL' is correct.

findings.claim: Final length: |JL| = sqrt(355/113 - 1/44^4) exactly; relative side error -6.04e-12
findings.detail: Recomputed via chained intersections only (nothing hardcoded): JL = 1.7724538508948128038 (20 digits; 30 digits 1.77245385089481280375890373052). JL - sqrt(q*) = 0 at 60 dps; JL^2 = 3.1415926535518512989 = q* = 1330573967/423534848 (fraction check residual 3e-61). sqrt(pi) = 1.7724538509055160273; JL - sqrt(pi) = -1.07032235393e-11; relative error JL/sqrt(pi)-1 = -6.03864723124e-12, matching the prior-verified -6.038647231e-12. q*-pi = -3.79419395584e-11 -> 10.42 absolute decimal digits of pi, so 'about 10.4 correct decimal digits' is accurate. 44^4 = 1936^2 = 3748096 confirmed.

findings.claim: Stroke count: total <= 28
findings.detail: Base 13 + Step1 line BC (1) + Step2 (0, both objects pre-drawn) + Step3 c(E,PX) (1) + Step4 line DC (1) + parallel#1 (3) + Step5 line DU (1) + parallel#2 (3) + Step6 c(W,BV) (1) + parallel#3 (3) + Step7 c(W,WI) (1) = 28 exactly. Meets the claim with zero slack, under the stated metric (rigid compass transfer = 1 stroke; parallel expanded to 2 circles + 1 line). Every parallel expansion was checked feasible: reference-line points and the external point are all constructed, and the external point is off the reference line in all three cases.

findings.claim: Nothing used before it exists; no undrawn object assumed drawn
findings.detail: Full dependency audit clean. Step 1 uses only base circles c(B,BO), c(Q,QO); step 2 uses base circle c(P,PB) and the given drawn diameter; step 4 intersects with line BC drawn in step 1; step 6 uses base line OW and the base-drawn diameter for the parallel; step 7's radius WI uses points W, I which both exist (undrawn segment WI never needed as an object). Each 'parallel through a point' is honestly expanded rather than assumed primitive; 'mark J' is costed as a circle stroke. The only unstated items are the three intra-expansion parallelogram branch choices (figure-resolvable) and the harmless J/L side choices.

sim.summary: Full numerical simulation at 50+ digits (mpmath, 60 dps working, /tmp/audit-cg28/audit.py) reproduces every claimed point from primitive intersections alone: C=(1,1), E=(-3,0), D=(-9/2,0), U=(1,1/44), V=(1,1/1936), J=(0,sqrt(3)-+1/1936), L=(+-sqrt(355/113-1/44^4), y_J). Final |JL| = 1.7724538508948128038, identically sqrt(355/113 - 1/44^4) (zero residual at 60 dps), vs sqrt(pi)=1.7724538509055160273; relative side error -6.03864723124e-12 matches the prior-verified value. All existence conditions hold with margin: the two unit circles genuinely meet at a second point distinct from O (center distance sqrt(2) strictly between 0 and 2); both G-parallels genuinely cross line BC (cross product 11/2 != 0 each time); c(W,WI) reaches the horizontal through J with radius/offset ratio ~3431. Selectors: 'beyond E' at step 3 is the one load-bearing choice and it is stated and unambiguous (wrong branch degrades the error to -9.5e-7); C, E selectors are stated and doubly resolved; J above/below and L left/right are unstated but proven harmless-by-symmetry (all four branch combinations give identical JL to 60 digits). Dependency audit clean — nothing is used before it exists, undrawn segment WI is never needed as an object, and parallels/distance-transfers are honestly costed. Stroke count is exactly 28 (13 base + 15), meeting the <=28 claim with zero slack. Sole minor caveat: the two-point branch choice inside each 3-stroke parallelogram expansion (parallel vs crossed antiparallel) is left implicit, though figure-resolvable.

findings.claim: C=(1,1) is the second intersection of c(B,BO) and c(Q,QO); |BC|=r; line BC is x=1, tangent to GAMMA at B
findings.detail: Exact solve gives intersections {(0,0),(1,1)}; O is the first, C=(1,1) the second. |BC|=1 exactly. The two circles are transversal (center distance sqrt(2), strictly between 0 and 2), so C is well-defined. Tangency of x=1 to GAMMA at B causes no problem: the line is drawn through two distinct constructed points, and all later intersections on it (U, V from non-vertical parallels) are transversal.

findings.claim: E=(-3,0) is a free intersection (c(P,PB) with the given diameter line); BE=4r
findings.detail: c(P,PB) has radius exactly 2; x-axis intersections are x=1 (B) and x=-3 (E). Both objects already drawn, so E costs 0 strokes. BE=4 exactly.

findings.claim: D=(-9/2,0) via c(E,PX), a rigid-compass transfer; BD=11r/2
findings.detail: PX=3/2 exactly; E is not an endpoint of PX (endpoints P=(-1,0), X=(1/2,0)), so this IS a distance transfer -- legal at 1 stroke under the stated rigid-compass metric. c(E,3/2) meets the axis at -9/2 and -3/2; 'beyond E' selects D=(-9/2,0); BD=11/2 exactly.

findings.claim: Parallel through G to DC meets BC at U=(1,1/44), BU = BC*BG/BD = 1/44, U on the correct side
findings.detail: B,G,D collinear on y=0; B,U,V,C collinear on x=1. Exact computation: line through G=(7/8,0) with direction C-D=(11/2,1) hits x=1 at (1,1/44). BG/BD=(1/8)/(11/2)=1/44 exactly. G strictly between B and D implies U strictly between B and C (0<1/44<1) -- correct side. G is not on line DC (signed area -43/8 != 0), so the parallel is a genuine distinct line and the parallelogram expansion is non-degenerate.

findings.claim: Parallel through G to DU meets BC at V=(1,1/1936) with BV/BU = BG/BD via triangles B-G-V ~ B-D-U
findings.detail: The stated similarity is the correct one: correspondence B<->B, G<->D, V<->U, with GV parallel to DU and the shared angle at B, giving BV/BU = BG/BD = 1/44. Exact coordinates confirm: direction U-D=(11/2,1/44), line through G hits x=1 at (1,1/1936). BV = 1/1936 = 1/44^2 exactly, and V lies between B and U.

findings.claim: J on OW with WJ=BV=1/1936; horizontal through J; L on c(W,WI) gives right angle at J and JL^2 = WI^2 - WJ^2 = 355/113 - 1/44^4 exactly, both J branches
findings.detail: WI^2 = 3 + 16/113 = 355/113 exactly. c(W,BV) is a legal rigid-compass transfer (1 stroke); it meets line OW at J=(0, sqrt(3) +/- 1/1936). For BOTH branches sympy gives WJ=1/1936, (J-W).(L-J)=0 (right angle: JW vertical, JL horizontal), and JL^2 = 1330573967/423534848 exactly. The horizontal line always meets c(W,WI) since WJ=1/1936 < WI ~ 1.77.

findings.claim: JL = sqrt(q*) r with q* = 1330573967/423534848; area relative error ~ -1.2e-11, side relative error ~ -6.04e-12
findings.detail: 355/113 - 1/44^4 = 1330573967/423534848 exactly (fraction in lowest terms, gcd=1; 44^4=1936^2=3748096). At 60-digit precision: q* = 3.14159265355185129890..., q*-pi = -3.7942e-11 (10.42 correct decimal places of pi), JL^2/(pi r^2)-1 = -1.20773e-11 (area), sqrt(q*/pi)-1 = -6.03865e-12 (side). All match the claimed figures, including the prior-audit value -6.038647231e-12.

findings.claim: Total <= 28 strokes
findings.detail: Base 13 + extension 15 = 28 exactly: line BC (1), c(E,PX) transfer (1), line DC (1), parallel-to-DC through G expanded via parallelogram method (2 transfer circles + 1 line = 3), line DU (1), parallel-to-DU through G (3), c(W,BV) transfer (1), parallel-to-OB through J (3), c(W,WI) (1, collapsing-legal since W is an endpoint). E, C, D, U, V, J, L are all free intersections. Every parallelogram expansion uses constructed points, involves only rigid-compass transfers (allowed at 1 stroke each), and is non-degenerate (the three point triples D-C-G, D-U-G, O-B-J are each non-collinear). Caveat: the verbatim prose treats 'draw a parallel' as one action; the 28 total only holds when each of the three parallels is charged its full 3-stroke expansion -- which is exactly what the metric requires, and the budget still closes at 28, not below.

geom.summary: Every geometric claim verifies under exact symbolic arithmetic. C=(1,1), E=(-3,0), D=(-9/2,0), U=(1,1/44), V=(1,1/1936) are all exactly as claimed; both similarity applications are correctly oriented (BGU~BDC then BGV~BDU, each giving ratio BG/BD=1/44); c(E,PX) and c(W,BV) are genuine rigid-compass transfers, legal at 1 stroke under the stated metric; both J branches yield WJ=1/1936, a right angle at J, and JL^2 = 355/113 - 1/44^4 = 1330573967/423534848 = q* exactly (lowest terms). Numerically (60 digits): q*-pi = -3.794e-11 (10.42 correct decimal places), area relative error -1.2077e-11, side relative error -6.0386e-12 -- matching the claimed -1.2e-11 and -6.04e-12. Stroke count closes at exactly 28 = 13 (base) + 15 (extension), with the three prose 'parallels' each expanded to the required 3 primitive strokes (2 transfer circles + 1 line, all non-degenerate) and all named points arising as free intersections. The tangency of line BC to GAMMA at B is incidental and causes no degeneracy. Verdict: valid, with the single presentational caveat that the verbatim steps gloss the mandatory 3-stroke expansion of each parallel (the arithmetic already accounts for it) and that the bound is attained with equality, 28, not slack.

findings.claim: Step 1: C=(1,1) is a free existing intersection; line BC costs 1 stroke
findings.detail: c(B,BO): (x-1)^2+y^2=1 and c(Q,QO): x^2+(y-1)^2=1 (both among the 8 drawn circles) intersect at O=(0,0) and C=(1,1). Line BC is x=1, tangent to GAMMA at B, and is load-bearing later (it catches U and V). Charge: 1 stroke, not a transfer.

findings.claim: Step 2: E=(-3,0) is free (0 strokes)
findings.detail: c(P,PB) (center (-1,0), r=2) meets the diameter line at x=1 (B) and x=-3 (E). Free intersection, BUT it requires the diameter beyond P (see infinite-line finding). BE = 4 confirmed.

findings.claim: Step 3: c(E,PX) costs 1 stroke and is a rigid-compass transfer
findings.detail: PX = 3/2, taken from the remote segment P-X (E is not an endpoint) — a genuine distance transfer. D = (-9/2,0) chosen beyond E; BD = 11/2. No transfer-free alternative exists: no constructed point lies at distance 3/2 from E (EO=3, EP=2, EX=7/2).

findings.claim: Step 4: line DC (1) + parallel through G = 3 primitive strokes; U=(1,1/44)
findings.detail: DC has slope 2/11; parallel through G=(7/8,0) meets x=1 at exactly (1,1/44); BU/BC = BG/BD = (1/8)/(11/2) = 1/44 verified. Cheapest legal expansion found: parallelogram method, c(G,|DC|) + c(C,|DG|) + line G-M with M=G+(C-D)=(43/8,1) — 3 strokes, BOTH circles transfers. No cheaper route exists: no drawn line is perpendicular to DC (kills the tangency trick), no drawn circle passes through a second point of the parallel, and an exhaustive check of 1- and 2-line central-projection routes between the drawn verticals (x=0, 4/sqrt(113), 1/2, 7/8, 1) from all axis points (D,E,P,O,I,X,G,B) yields no product of ratios equal to 1/44 (required centers land at s=337/336, 79/78, 36/35 — none constructed). Step total: 4.

findings.claim: Step 5: line DU (1) + parallel through G = 3 strokes; V=(1,1/1936)
findings.detail: DU slope 1/242; parallel through G meets x=1 at (1, 1/1936) exactly; BV/BU = BG/BD = 1/44, so BV = 1/44^2 = 1/1936. Same analysis as step 4: parallelogram expansion c(G,|DU|) + c(U,|DG|) + line = 3 strokes (2 transfers); no cheaper legal expansion found. Step total: 4.

findings.claim: Step 6a: 'mark J so that WJ = BV' is one transfer circle, not free
findings.detail: J requires circle c(W,BV): center W, radius = distance B-V, W not an endpoint — a rigid-compass transfer, 1 stroke. J = (0, sqrt(3) -/+ 1/1936) on drawn line OW (either intersection works; right angle at J holds for both). It IS charged in the 28 total.

findings.claim: Step 6b: parallel through J to OB — naive 3 strokes, but a legal 2-stroke expansion exists (tangency trick)
findings.detail: Naive parallelogram (translate O->B): c(J,|OB|) + c(B,|OJ|) + line = 3 strokes, 2 transfers. Cheaper legal expansion: J lies exactly on x=0, and the drawn 'chord x=1/2' is listed among the DRAWN LINES, so dist(J, that line) = 1/2 = OX exactly. Circle c(J,OX) (1 transfer stroke) is tangent to line x=1/2 at (1/2, y_J) — a single, exactly-determined intersection point ON the desired horizontal; line J-(1/2,y_J) (1 stroke) completes it. Total 2 strokes. Caveats: (i) requires 'chord x=1/2' to be an infinite line, since y_J ~ 1.7315 is outside GAMMA (chord segment tops out at 0.866) — but the same infinite-line convention is already required for E and D; (ii) requires accepting a tangency point as a free intersection. If either is rejected, fall back to 3 strokes. Note also a 3-stroke ZERO-transfer alternative (Thales perpendicular at J on OW using existing C=(1,1)): c(C,CJ) [collapsing-legal] -> second meet J'=(0,2-y_J) with OW -> line J'C meets the circle again at Z=(2,y_J) -> line JZ. Relevant only for the strict-collapsing census.

findings.claim: Step 7: c(W,WI) is 1 stroke, collapsing-legal; JL needs no extra stroke
findings.detail: W is an endpoint of WI (both W and I constructed; the segment need not be drawn), so this is not a transfer. L = c(W,WI) ∩ (drawn horizontal through J); J and L both lie on that drawn line, so the answer segment JL is already drawn — 0 extra strokes. JL^2 = WI^2 - WJ^2 = 355/113 - 1/44^4 = q* by the right angle at J.

findings.claim: Accuracy: JL^2 = q* = 1330573967/423534848, relative side error -6.04e-12 (~10.4 digits of pi)
findings.detail: Independently recomputed: 44^4 = 1936^2 = 3748096; q* = 3.14159265355185129890..., q* - pi = -3.794e-11, sqrt(q*/pi) - 1 = -6.0386472e-12, -log10|q*-pi| = 10.42. Matches the prior verified facts exactly.

findings.claim: Claimed total <= 28 strokes
findings.detail: Naive charge list: step1 BC=1, step3 c(E,PX)=1, step4 DC+parallel=4, step5 DU+parallel=4, step6 c(W,BV)+parallel=4, step7 c(W,WI)=1 -> extension = 15; 13 base + 15 = 28 EXACTLY. So <=28 is honest and tight under the naive parallelogram expansion — no hidden free 'parallels'. With the tangency shortcut in step 6 the extension drops to 14 and the best legal total is 27 (13+14). Nothing cheaper was found for the step-4/5 parallels, so 27 is my floor under generous legality, 28 under conservative legality.

findings.claim: Transfer census
findings.detail: Base 13 strokes contain ZERO transfers (all 8 base circles are collapsing-legal: center is an endpoint of the radius segment). Extension, naive (15 strokes, 9 circles + 6 lines): 8 of 9 circles are transfers — c(E,PX); c(G,|DC|), c(C,|DG|) [step 4]; c(G,|DU|), c(U,|DG|) [step 5]; c(W,BV); c(J,|OB|), c(B,|OJ|) [step 6 parallelogram]. Only c(W,WI) is collapsing-legal. Generous 27-stroke variant: 7 transfers (tangency circle c(J,OX) replaces the two step-6 transfer circles). Transfer-minimizing variant (Thales for step 6, 15 extension strokes): 6 transfers. Strict-collapsing-compass estimate at +6 primitives per transfer (Euclid I.2): naive 28 + 8*6 = 76; generous 27 + 7*6 = 69; best mixed strategy (Thales step 6, 6 transfers) 28 + 6*6 = 64 total primitives.

findings.claim: E and D lie outside segment PB — infinite-line/producibility assumption
findings.detail: E at x=-3 and D at x=-4.5 require the diameter to exist (or be freely producible, Euclid Post. 2) out to x=-4.5. This is CONSISTENT with the stated base: the given is 'the drawn diameter LINE through P, O, B', and the base construction itself never intersects the diameter outside [-1,1] (W comes from c(P,2)∩c(B,2), K from c(Q,1)∩c(G,7/8), H from c(O,1/2)∩line OK — all verified), so no contradiction, but the extension is the first place the infinite-line convention is load-bearing. The same convention legitimizes the 2-stroke tangency trick (uses line x=1/2 at height 1.73). Flag noted, not a defect.

count.summary: Verdict on '<= 28 strokes': HONEST, tight-but-fair — not an undercount. Every step's geometry checks out exactly (C=(1,1), D=(-9/2,0), U=(1,1/44), V=(1,1/1936), JL^2 = 355/113 - 1/44^4 = q*, relative side error -6.04e-12, ~10.4 digits of pi). Charging every primitive honestly with the naive parallelogram expansion for all three parallels gives extension = 15 strokes (1 BC + 1 c(E,PX) + 4 step-4 + 4 step-5 + 4 step-6 + 1 c(W,WI)), i.e. 13 + 15 = 28 exactly — the claim's bound is met with zero slack and no smuggled free parallels; 'mark J' is correctly a charged transfer circle and JL genuinely needs no extra stroke. My own best total is 27 (13 + 14): the step-6 horizontal through J admits a legal 2-stroke expansion because J sits exactly on the y-axis at distance 1/2 from the drawn line x=1/2 — circle c(J,OX) is tangent to that line at (1/2,y_J), giving the second point, then one line (accepting a tangency point as a free intersection and the infinite-line convention that steps 2-3 already require). The step-4/5 parallels cannot be beaten below 3 strokes each (no perpendicular drawn line for the tangency trick, no projection shortcut to ratio 1/44 exists among the constructed points — verified exhaustively). Transfer census: 0 transfers in the base; 8 transfers in the naive extension (7 in the 27-stroke variant; 6 minimum via a zero-transfer Thales perpendicular for step 6), giving strict-collapsing estimates of 76 / 69 / 64 (best) primitives at +6 per transfer. Caveat flags: E=(-3,0) and D=(-9/2,0) require the diameter as an infinite drawn line (consistent with the stated base, which calls it a LINE and never needed it outside [-1,1] before); this convention is load-bearing for the first time in the extension.

findings.claim: q* = 355/113 - 1/44^4 gives |q*-pi| = 3.794194e-11, i.e. 10.42 correct decimal digits of pi
findings.detail: mpmath (60 dps): q*-pi = -3.794194e-11; -log10|err| = 10.4209. Cross-check err/(2pi) = -6.0386472e-12 matches the audited side relative error -6.0386e-12 exactly. 44^4 = 3748096, 1/44^4 = 2.66802e-7 vs excess 355/113-pi = 2.66764e-7.

findings.claim: Per-stroke economy beats Ramanujan 1914 and Chu 2019 by 2.2-2.8x
findings.detail: Candidate: 10.42 digits / 28-34 strokes = 0.306-0.372 digits/stroke. Ramanujan 1914: 8/58 = 0.138. Chu: 9/>=68 <= 0.132. Candidate strictly dominates both (fewer strokes AND more digits).

findings.claim: Candidate dominates the published frontier at its accuracy class for any honest count in 28-34
findings.detail: Table (strokes / digits / dig-per-stroke): Beatrix 13 / ~4 / 0.31; ChatGPT-14 14 / 6.57 / 0.47; Ramanujan-1913 24-38 / 6.57 / 0.17-0.27; candidate 28-34 / 10.42 / 0.31-0.37; Ramanujan-1914 ~58 / 8.0 / 0.14; Chu >=68 / 9.0 / <=0.13. Nothing published at <=38 strokes exceeds 6.6 digits; everything above 8 digits costs >=58 strokes. Candidate is a new Pareto point even at 34. Note it does not beat ChatGPT-14 on raw digits/stroke (0.47), but that is a lower accuracy class; both remain frontier points.

findings.claim: 1/44^4 is a numerological near-hit, not continued-fraction structure
findings.detail: 1/44^4 = 2.66802e-7 matches the needed excess 2.66764e-7 to relative accuracy 1.42e-4; the next CF convergent of pi after 355/113 is 103993/33102, unrelated to 44^4. Caveat should be carried.

findings.claim: Ramanujan 1913 footnote (355/113)(1-0.0003/3533) is ~34800x more accurate than q*, and has no comparably cheap intercept chain
findings.detail: Footnote error = 1.0901e-15 (14.96 digits); ratio |q* err|/|footnote err| = 34805.7. Correction ratio 3/35330000 with 35330000 = 2^4 * 5^4 * 3533 and 3533 PRIME; sqrt(3/35330000) -> 1/3431.71, not a nice rational, so no two-equal-small-ratio chain (no '1/44 twice' analog). A double-intercept realization needs a 4-digit prime mark (3533) plus 10^4 scaling: roughly double the candidate's correction machinery, est. ~45-60 strokes for ~15 digits = 0.25-0.33 digits/stroke. q* stays the equal-or-better strokes-per-digit deal at far lower absolute stroke cost.

findings.claim: Headline: 'about 7031x more accurate than Ramanujan-1913 at roughly double the strokes'
findings.detail: The 7031x is exact: |355/113 - pi| / |q* - pi| = 7030.85. But 'roughly double the strokes' only holds against the 14-stroke ChatGPT-355/113 construction (28-34 = 2.0-2.4 x 14). Against Ramanujan's OWN 1913 construction, counted by Beatrix at 24-38 strokes, the candidate sits at comparable or even fewer strokes - the headline oversells the cost there. Fair phrasing: '7031x more accurate than 355/113 at roughly double the strokes of the leanest 355/113 construction, and at par with Ramanujan's own 1913 stroke count.'

frontier.summary: All context arithmetic verifies at 60-digit precision: q* = 355/113 - 1/44^4 misses pi by 3.794194e-11 (10.42 decimal digits; side relative error -6.0386e-12 confirmed), is exactly 7030.85x more accurate than 355/113, and at any honest count in 28-34 strokes it strictly dominates Ramanujan 1914 (8 digits @ ~58) and Chu (9 digits @ >=68) - more digits at roughly half the strokes, 2.2-2.8x better digits/stroke. Caveats to carry: 1/44^4 is a numerological near-hit (1.4e-4 relative match, no CF structure), and Ramanujan's 1913 footnote correction 3/35330000 (34806x more accurate, 14.96 digits) cannot be built with a comparably cheap chain because 35330000 = 2^4*5^4*3533 with 3533 prime - estimated ~45-60 strokes for ~15 digits, leaving q* the better strokes-per-digit deal. The headline's '7031x' is exact, but 'roughly double the strokes' is only fair versus the 14-stroke 355/113 construction; versus Ramanujan's own 1913 construction (24-38 strokes) the candidate is at par or cheaper, so the headline actually understates the stroke economy - valid with that framing caveat.
